dimensions.note: Report-only: pure data shaping over workflow artifacts, no credentials, no AWS API, no secrets. The bundle is SDK-free — it imports only the pure ./_lib/scan_error.ts twin (zod-only) and never receives the SDK-bearing aws_credentials.ts twin; verified no aws-sdk/smithy import in the report dir.
All logging goes through the never-throws tryLog wrapper with structured placeholders; no new log lines added in this slice and none carry sensitive data.

Unchanged never-throws posture: a bad-JSON or schema-mismatched artifact is counted into skipped, a getContent read failure is a per-handle skip, and an unexpected collection failure degrades to a still-valid report with degraded=true. The fix here is the opposite of swallowing: a `network` scan_error row now decodes (canonical schema) rather than being silently dropped into skipped as malformed.

New cases driven through the decode seam (synthetic rows): a network row with a service tag decodes (not skipped); a legacy row WITHOUT a service field still parses with service==''; renderMarkdown emits the transient-network section and surfaces service/region; report.execute errorsByKind now counts the network bucket. Existing skip/degrade tests retained.
Report-only extension; it renders collected workflow data and creates/updates/deletes no external resources, so idempotency does not apply.

No external API client; the report consumes swamp data artifacts only. It validates every decoded artifact with safeParse before use (the canonical ScanErrorSchema is strict on kind), so artifact shape is checked, not assumed.
No file handles, sockets, or temp files; a single TextDecoder reused across decodes. No new resources introduced by the schema-import change.

manifest version bumped to 2026.06.26.1 (report has no model.version to align). The generated _lib/scan_error.ts twin carries the auto-generated do-not-edit header, and its body below that header is byte-identical to the canonical source (drift gate clean). No real account IDs / IPs / hostnames in source, README, or fixtures.
